tree.setup: The entrance of a movie theater, 7:15 PM. Your friend Xiao Yang has been waiting since 7:00. He's standing with his arms crossed, tapping one foot. He's not actually angry -- he's just dramatic. He's been rehearsing his disappointed face in the glass door.
tree.setup_zh: 电影院门口，晚上七点一刻。你朋友小杨从七点就在等了。他双手交叉站着，一只脚在打拍子。他其实没生气——只是有点夸张。他在玻璃门上练了好几遍失望的表情。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: nǐ lái le! wǒ děng le hěn jiǔ le!
turns.text_en: You're here! I've been waiting forever!

options.feedback: Xiao Yang drops the disappointed face immediately -- he can't maintain it longer than five seconds. He grins.「没事没事！」对不起 is the standard apology. Clean and direct.

options.text_pinyin: bù hǎoyìsi, lùshang rén hěn duō.
options.text_en: Sorry, there were lots of people on the way.
options.feedback: Xiao Yang nods sagely -- traffic is a universally accepted excuse in Chinese cities. 不好意思 is softer than 对不起, perfect for minor social infractions.
Xiao Yang's eyes widen. Playing innocent when you're clearly late is a bold strategy. He appreciates the audacity, if not the punctuality.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 没关系！快走吧，电影要开始了！
turns.text_pinyin: méi guānxi! kuài zǒu ba, diànyǐng yào kāishǐ le!
turns.text_en: It's fine! Let's go, the movie is about to start!

options.feedback: You both hurry through the doors. Xiao Yang has already forgiven you -- he was mostly just glad to have someone to see the movie with. 走吧 is the universal 'let's go.'
Xiao Yang is genuinely touched. Nobody has ever thanked him for waiting. He files this away as a reason you're a good friend.
Xiao Yang stares at you. You are fifteen minutes late and now you want to stop for snacks. His left eye twitches. But... he does want popcorn.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: xià cì zǎo diǎn lái a!

tree.cultural_note: Chinese people value punctuality (准时, zhǔnshí), but a few minutes late for social events is usually forgiven with a quick 对不起 or 不好意思. Blaming traffic or subway crowds is always understood.
